methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $85M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Mariana Minerals is a Series A-stage climate and energy company that has raised $85M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of nine private companies and one public comparable (LNZA), with primary focus on three same-sector, same-stage private peers: Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, using total raised as a proxy baseline, applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A progression, implying a $170M valuation. The peer-median method across the three Climate & Energy Series A comparables yields a $150M valuation, with the peer set ranging from $110M to $200M. These two approaches triangulate to a median valuation of $160M, with a supported range of $128M (low) to $195M (high).

The $160M median reflects the convergence of forward-looking stage dynamics and current peer market positioning. Key uncertainties center on company-specific execution risk and the volatility of relative valuations within the broader climate tech cohort, particularly given the $90M spread observed across the peer sample. Additional diligence on revenue traction, capital efficiency, and competitive positioning would inform placement within the derived range.
